coreSkills: Query optimization (INNER JOIN / LEFT OUTER JOIN)
domainKnowledge: Construction technology tools and estimating workflows
Product catalog data models (tbl_products, tbl_product_variants, tbl_assemblies)
softSkills: Clear communication and ability to explain technical choices
Ability to deliver a concise, high‑impact solution within a short timeline
Comfortable working directly via terminal on Linux environments
summary: A short‑term contract for an experienced Senior Full‑Stack Developer to resolve critical backend performance and memory bottlenecks in a construction‑technology SaaS application. The work involves refactoring eager‑loading queries, implementing server‑side pagination, building lightweight autocomplete search endpoints, updating Vue.js UI components, and tuning Node.js/V8 memory limits under PM2 on Linux. Strong proficiency in Node.js (Express/NestJS), relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL via Sequelize), Vue.js, Redis, and Linux/PM2 is essential.

prioritySignals.signal: Proven experience fixing Node.js/V8 heap out‑of‑memory crashes and memory leaks
prioritySignals.reason: The primary pain point is OOM crashes caused by unbounded eager loading; fixing these directly addresses the contract’s core objective.

prioritySignals.signal: Deep expertise in relational database query optimization (PostgreSQL/MySQL, Sequelize)
prioritySignals.reason: Root cause identified in INNER JOIN/LEFT OUTER JOIN queries on tbl_products, tbl_product_variants, and tbl_assemblies; optimizing these is central to the work.
All refactoring, pagination, and API creation must be implemented in the backend Node environment.

prioritySignals.signal: Vue.js frontend experience with async state management
prioritySignals.reason: Frontend dropdowns and tables must be updated to consume the new paginated APIs and handle real‑time user input.
Redis caching and background job handling are mentioned as part of the stack, supporting overall performance improvements.
Process memory limits and execution options are managed via PM2 on Linux; hands‑on terminal work is required.

prioritySignals.signal: Ability to deliver a concise, high‑impact solution within 2‑3 days (16‑20 hours)
prioritySignals.reason: The contract is short‑term; quick, focused execution is critical for meeting client expectations.
